title: Podman Pod loses network connectivity after some time
body:
### Issue Description

I am setting up a number of containers (grafana and prometheus) in a pod on Oracle Linux 9. After some time (2-3? days), the outbound connectivity stops working and both Prometheus and Grafana (running in separate containers) are not unable to scrape data from other machines.

Stop/starting the pod works, but this seems like a bug of some sort.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set up grafana and prometheus containers in a pod and leave running
2. A few days later, none of the containers are able to access external servers. ssh, ping, curl/wget, etc are all blocked. 
3. podman pod my-pod stop; podman pod my-pod start fixes the issue.


### Describe the results you received

Lost network connectivity after a few days.

### Describe the results you expected

Network connectivity to continue working.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

Client:       Podman Engine

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64


### Additional information

Has occurred 2 times now that we are aware of, and suspect a third occasion.

comments.body: Can you provide an exact reproducer? How are the pods/containers created.

It's basically all deployed from ansible. Not so easy to extract the code (I'd have to extract the secrets), but it's basically just ansible commands to deploy the pod + the five containers:

```
- name: Create pod grafana-pod
    containers.podman.podman_pod:
    name: "grafana-pod"
    state: created
    infra_name: "grafana-infra"
    infra_image: "{{ docker_repository }}/rancher/pause:3.6"
    publish:
        - "{{ grafana_port }}:{{ grafana_port }}"
        - "{{ blackbox_exporter_port }}:{{ blackbox_exporter_port }}"
        - "{{ prometheus_port }}:{{ prometheus_port }}"
    become_user: "grafana"
    become: yes

- name: Create postgres container
    containers.podman.podman_container:
    name: "grafana-postgres"
    image: "{{ docker_repository }}/postgres:15.2"
    env: "{{ postgres_environment }}"
    volumes:
        - "{{ postgres_volume_mount }}"
    pod: "grafana-pod"
    restart_policy: always
    become_user: "grafana"
    become: yes

- name: Create prometheus container
    containers.podman.podman_container:
    name: "prometheus"
    image: "{{ docker_repository }}/prom/prometheus/v2.43.0"
    command: "{% for key in prometheus_commandline_args %}--{{key}}={{prometheus_commandline_args[key]}} {% endfor %}"
    volumes:
        - "{{ prometheus_bind_mount }}"
        - "{{ prometheus_volume_mount }}"
    pod: "grafana-pod"
    restart_policy: always
    become_user: "grafana"
    become: yes

- name: Create blackbox-exporter container
    containers.podman.podman_container:
    name: "blackbox-exporter"
    image: "{{ docker_repository }}/prom/blackbox-exporter:v0.23.0"
    command: "{% for key in blackbox_exporter_commandline_args %}--{{key}}={{blackbox_exporter_commandline_args[key]}} {% endfor %}"
    volumes:
        - "{{ blackbox_exporter_bind_mount }}"
    pod: "grafana-pod"
    restart_policy: always
    become_user: "grafana"
    become: yes

- name: Create grafana container
    containers.podman.podman_container:
    name: "grafana"
    image: "{{ docker_repository }}/grafana/grafana-oss:9.5.1"
    volumes: "{{ grafana_mount_volumes }}"
    pod: "grafana-pod"
    restart_policy: always
    become_user: "grafana"
    become: yes
```

With some previous code to set up the VM (adding certificates, etc) + setting up the users and volumes for the containers (also using ansible). It's relatively straightforward stuff.

Sorry this isn't something I can work with.

The first think that looks of is the `runRoot: /tmp/podman-run-1001/containers`. If you are on a systemd distro that should use `/run/user/$UID/containers`. Please run `podman system reset` then make sure you are logged into the user properly, i.e. do not use sudo or su to switch users. see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#31-podman-run-fails-with-erro0000-xdg_runtime_dir-directory-runuser0-is-not-owned-by-the-current-user-or-error-creating-tmpdir-mkdir-runuser1000-permission-denied

Besides that you should check if the slirp4netns process for the pod is still running. Hoe does the cotnianer netns look like when the issue occurs, for example check `podman exec <contianer> ip addr`.

Thanks - I think this set me on the right track wrt understanding the problem. Spent some time diving down the rabbithole of ansible/podman, and the problem with ansible is of course that it basically uses sudo. So "logging in properly" is easier said than done. But doing this without ansible is really not an option for us.

Will do some more digging and get back.

Some national holidays interrupting work, but back to looking at this now. As mentioned, the problem with using ansible for scripting this is that there is not really any way one can log into the user "properly"; ansible uses su, and that's pretty hard to fix. 

Network state is unknown when connectivity is lost:
```
    1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    2: tap0: <BROADCAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65520 qdisc fq_codel state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
```
Comes back up on a restart.

Working theory is that it's the /tmp location that is the problem, since slirp4netns basically looks like this:
`   /bin/slirp4netns --disable-host-loopback --mtu=65520 --enable-sandbox --enable-seccomp --enable-ipv6 -c -e 3 -r 4 --netns-type=path /tmp/podman-run-1003/netns/netns-ec02c9a9-c51d-1f78-7574-42d39f0f767b tap0`

Well, fixed the rundir issues by setting XDG_RUNTIME_DIR this explicitly in ansible podman (so runRoot is now /run/user/1003/containers as expected); but the connectivity stil dissappears after at most a couple of days.

slirp is running and I can't find any obvious differences between network states while there is an issue, and after I've run a podman pod restart.

Stumped by this. Any further suggestions as to what I can look into?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

The interface state is always shown as UNKNOWN so that does not say anything.
If you use podman 4.4 you can try `--network pasta` instead of slirp4netns.

If slirp4netns is still running I have no idea, could be a bug in the process. Maybe attach `strace` and see if it shows anything different compared to when it works.

Not crazy about your setup either.
  runRoot: /tmp/podman-run-1001/containers

Systemd has been known to cause havok with this type of setup.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we gave you workarounds and suggested what could cause the issue and have not heard back, closing.

Been on vacation during the summer, which is the reason I've not been able to follow up. Will try pasta next. If that doesn't work, I guess we'll just have to drop podman as not being ready for production usage on OL9.
This is a closed issue, if you are having issues, please open a new one.
